hawkish_phrases: a small margin of excess demand was projected to emerge by early 2020 and build thereafter
an ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period will be appropriate
The steady absorption of slack has reduced the degree to which it is appropriate for the MPC to accommodate an extended period of inflation above the target
Inflation remained above the 2% target in the second and third years of the MPC's central projection
pay growth will rise further in response to the tightening labour market
dovish_phrases: any future increases in Bank Rate are likely to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
GDP was expected to grow by around 1¾% per year on average over the forecast period. While modest by historical standards
CPI inflation fell from 3.0% in January to 2.7% in February
Inflation is expected to ease further in the short term
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.5%, with explicit forward guidance that ongoing tightening over the forecast period will be appropriate to address projected excess demand and above-target inflation.
